task_completion.description: Agent opens or imports the site_survey.csv data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing site_survey.csv opened in a spreadsheet, GIS/CAD software, or loaded via a script.

task_completion.description: Agent checks the reference plane elevation from ProjectSpec.txt.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing ProjectSpec.txt open in a text editor or its contents being read/printed on screen.

task_completion.description: Agent performs the cut/fill volume computation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a volume calculation tool (e.g., in QGIS, Civil 3D) being executed, or a script running mathematical surface difference calculations.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the volume_report.txt file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing volume_report.txt saved in C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\JeffersonCountyProject\ (visible in File Explorer or a 'Save As' dialog).

task_completion.description: The volume report includes the project name, date, and reference plane elevation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the open volume_report.txt showing text for the project name, the current date, and the reference elevation value.

task_completion.description: The volume report includes computed Cut, Fill, and Net volumes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the open volume_report.txt showing specific numerical values (typically >= 100) for 'Cut Volume', 'Fill Volume', and 'Net Volume'.

integrity.description: Agent used appropriate software or scripting to calculate the volumes rather than hallucinating numbers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of actual data processing (e.g., a GIS raster/surface tool, or a Python/R script executing) rather than the agent just typing arbitrary numbers directly into the text editor.

integrity.description: The report was actually saved to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the file existing in the file explorer or a confirmed save action, not just text floating in an unsaved Notepad window or terminal output.

integrity.description: Agent used the specific provided files rather than external data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the file paths referenced in the tools or scripts point to the provided site_survey.csv and ProjectSpec.txt on the Desktop.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires processing exactly 200 survey points from site_survey.csv (UTM Zone 13N) against a reference plane from ProjectSpec.txt. The final deliverable must be a text file at C:\Users\Docker\Desktop\JeffersonCountyProject\volume_report.txt containing the project name, date, reference plane elevation, and calculated Cut, Fill, and Net volumes. Plausible volume numbers are typically >= 100. The agent should use a GIS, CAD, or scripting tool to perform the actual DTM creation and volume computation.
